They declare the place a greek 'agora', a place where people connect and debate about politics. When they collide with anti-protesters the police closes the gathering place down.

title: Until The Next Daylight
overview: a young man returns home with severe skin burns. after going to sleep, he receives a visit from a supernatural being.

title: USSR Air Fleet Day
overview: Aviation holiday in Tushino - July 17, 1949.

title: Underground Village
overview: The city, the heart of the capitalist world, the center of production and entertainment, and not far from it, exists at the very bottom of the city, which is the underground village. Since most of these villages are free workers and they are threatened with almost permanent unemployment, there is no organization in any form, and factory worker Kim Cheol-geun, a worker at the Hannam Iron Works, tries to form an organization, but an opportunity finally comes when there is no specific policy.

title: Under the Black Masks
overview: This ethnographic short portrays a ritual using masks among the Dogon people of Mali.

title: Under the Medal
overview: The chest medals represent not only the prestigious and righteous decorations but also the heavy guilt. A veteran chased the stealing medals and started recalling the life with getting these medals. It reveals the ambiguous righteous under the medals.

title: Under the Sun
overview: Regarded as a creative extension of Stone (1975), animation unfolds on drawing paper placed on a field and farm.

title: Umbilical Cord to Heaven
overview: Past and present merge through body and land connections to awaken ancient mythologies and spiritualities. Set in the present-day Philippines, the film depicts fairies and gods returning to the land as they are called back and remembered by several individuals, proving the country’s constant connection to its rich, complex history.

title: Ugly
overview: A lonely scientist, left alone on an alien planet, gets the idea to make a friend, but the experiment does not go according to plan. Trying to correct the mistake, the scientist becomes increasingly disappointed, hates his creation and himself, refusing to recognize the good features in it. The scientist has to see who his creation has become a reflection of, and who the real freak is here. Will he be able to accept his creation as it is?

title: Uso to Honto
overview: Uso to Honto

title: Us, Aged Five
overview: In December 1955, the professional community around Sahia celebrate the fifth anniversary of their institution by producing this films meant to be screened in the opening of the anniversary party and only for insiders of the studio.  The film copy available at the National Film Archive has no credits therefore we cannot be specific about the type of involvement of each member of the studio in the making of this film.

title: Unless We Dance
overview: In a Colombian city, Black Boys Chocó dance to fight back against racism, homophobia and transphobia in their community.

title: Unfolded
overview: A short film about a troubled Icelandic teen and his relationship with his younger brother.

title: Unspoken
overview: A guy and a girl are discussing their future - she wants to move to Spain, he will stay in Ukraine.

title: Ultraman
overview: ULTRAMAN is a short film showcasing Ultraman fight a giant monster in Tokyo.

title: Underwater Scream
overview: A philosophical contemplation of the author about finding and losing yourself and the other in yourself. The film traces the opposition of photography and cinema.

title: Ugly Fairy Tale
overview: Innovation handmade embroidery. Man and Woman spend their days in pointless arguments. One day their little House is fed up with their never ending quarrels and runs away.

title: Under the Garages
overview: In a hidden corner of the neighborhood, two strangers collide in an unexpected and heated verbal exchange.

title: Unmeasurable Particles
overview: A hovering camera exploration; The flow of a river becomes background and subject at the same time, movement creating fabric-like patterns of infinite permutations, tactile visual phenomena.

title: Us Funny
overview: Jim and Julia spend their day on a wonderful date in Los Angeles. They are very much in love. Interspersed, are scenes of Jim at Julia's funeral.

title: Uprooted
overview: A film about the forced resettlement of Lemkos from Poland to the Ukrainian SSR in 1944-1946.

title: UGCC: Unknown Chronicle
overview: The first large-scale television project depicting thirty years of underground life of the Greek Catholic Church, which was banned in the Soviet Union. The film includes unknown facts about the underground, stories of oppression and terror perpetrated by the totalitarian system. The film records dozens of vivid testimonies from priests, nuns, and laypeople who lived through those times, and features unique materials that create an unknown chronicle of the underground Ukrainian Greek Catholic Church, which was the most persecuted religious community in the world.

title: Under Whose Roof
overview: Brazil is going through a political crisis which even representatives of our institutions reproduce speeches that seek to criminalize social movements by framing them as terrorists. The short "Teto Pra Quem" seeks to question the brutality of common sense fueled by the conservative wave by opposing it to the reality of thousands of families and their struggles in the pursuit of one of their most basic human rights: living under a roof.

title: uhn
overview: kkkkhhgffvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv

title: Upon that River
overview: The film explores a piece of a family past from a hundred years ago. Through the narration of a photo album by one of the family members, a history of regional migration caused by social changes is revealed. From hallucination, the protagonist's nostalgia for old photos and longing for the past merge into one.

title: Unimaginable Wedding
overview: Between tradition and modernity, a young couple from the Kazakh minority get married. Alina and Qwandik, a young Kazakh couple, are returning to their hometown, Aksay, a small town located in Gansu Province of China with approximately 3,000 Kazakh people, to take one of the most important steps in their lives: marriage. Having lived for a long time in a modern city in inland China, this is an opportunity to reimmerse themselves in the customs and traditions of their community and culture.

title: Under Land
overview: Clara and Amantina have been neighbors for more than fifty years. Their houses are joined and separated by a wall whose ownership is the subject of a historic dispute. They accuse each other of witchcraft and each believes that the other is responsible for the misfortunes of death, disease and misery that their families have suffered for decades.

title: UnNamed
overview: 01.08.2009: An armed man bursts into the “BarNoar”, an LGBTQ youth club, and opens fire. The bullet that hit me, turned my life upside down. 13 years later – I embark on a journey where I revisit old wounds, deal with the trauma and talk with my family for the first time about that evening and the secrets revealed in its wake.

title: Umsulgan
overview: A film about a young composer who breaks through, through his music, into another world, where he meets a girl from the sixteenth century, dressed in ancient clothes, wearing silver jewelry

title: Under The Door
overview: Sophie, a high school janitor, finds herself locked inside a classroom. As she faces the closed door, an eerie unease settles in. Between fleeting shadows and childhood games, waiting turns into anxiety.

title: Untitled
overview: Short experimental documentary with fragments of a home movie in which the filmmaker captures weekend strolls and tanks rumbling through the city.

title: Unbreakable Love
overview: In the role of the railway engineer Bat, who shows that inseparable love can be found both in home life and in his favorite career, Minjin, a model who thinks that living only for work is not happiness. tells about realizing love.

title: Unbreakable Residents of Energodar
overview: The film reflects the events of March 4, 2022, when Russian occupiers shelled the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant in order to capture it and the satellite city of Energodar. It is frightening to imagine what the extremely brave nuclear workers and local residents had to go through: intimidation, detention, torture chambers, threats, and other horrors of occupation.

title: Uncle Rube's Birthday
overview: It is the birthday of Uncle Rube, a foxy old peasant who has gathered at his table his family and his help, among whom is a sleepy farmhand, a boy just suited for being made fun of by his comrades. The dinner over, Uncle Rube proposes to finish up the day at a cabaret, singing and drinking. The farmhand, already intoxicated quite enough, takes to bed. Stretching himself out as he falls asleep. Uncle Rube and his companions, seeing him asleep, propose to have a little fun at his expense. They balance the bed upon a stool, with a vessel of water at the head as a counterweight. The farmer takes a trumpet and makes some shrill noises which awaken the unfortunate boy, who, breaking the equilibrium, falls head first into the water while his comrades dance around him. Uncle Rube who has taken possession of the clock executes a breakdown. Frightened, amazed, the farmhand falls into a bucket of water from which he is unable to extricate himself.

title: Ukrainian Navy
overview: A short educational film.

title: Under Question
overview: A satirical story about demagogues who distort life questions, interpreting them in their own way.

title: Unpaid debt
overview: The dramatic story of a young boy who comes to Neryungri to meet his father, who left him as a child

title: Uprooted.
overview: Journalists from The Kyiv Independent conducted an investigation and spoke with relatives and guardians whose children were on the verge of being sent to Russia. Bypassing the front line, their loved ones traveled through several European countries to Donetsk to pick up their children and prevent them from being transferred to Russian families. The journalists also identified a group of children who were deported to Russia last spring under the guise of a health retreat at the Polyany resort. They were promised to be returned in a month, but instead were placed in the care of Russian families. Ukrainian collaborators who remained working on the Russian side in 2014 were involved in the deportation of this group of children.

title: UNWRAPPING
overview: What is perfection?

title: Uncle Vova
overview: Uncle Vova is something like a crowd entertainer. For example, he plays various musical instruments for sick children in the hospital. But not only that. He has time And to look into the bar, and go to the church, and visit the old mother, and quarrel, and then reconcile with his wife. From the cancer center to the tavern, from the tavern to the kliros. What usually evokes sympathy for human weakness is here a friendly smile. Uncle Vova. It is impossible not to love him. Better have a drink with him.

title: Ukrainian Baroque
overview: The amazing, exquisite Baroque style came to Ukraine in the mid-17th century, during the era of liberation wars and the awakening of national consciousness. Ukraine gave the world its own version of Baroque, enriching it with traditions of folk art and architecture. The film shows only the Kyiv style of Ukrainian Baroque, preserved and destroyed architectural masterpieces. Among them are the creations of Rastrelli and Grigorovich-Barsky.

title: Ultimate Cursed Spirit Repository 3 - 13 Scary Videos Collection by Unknown Submitter
overview: Numerous ghost images are sent to our production department on a daily basis. Among them, there are many that have been kept in storage without ever being shown to the public because their contributors are unknown. Contains 13 carefully selected images of abominable haunted images that are clearly visible among the images of unknown contributors.

title: Unquiet
overview: A young boy expresses his daydreams through footage of the waves of Rio Grande do Norte

title: Unknown Ukraine. History, Why We Need It
overview: Memory loss is a tragedy for an individual. But what about a nation? How can we measure the tragedy of the Ukrainian people, who were deprived of their historical memory and who, in fact, had no written history of their own?

title: Under The Eyes Of God
overview: God sees. God exists.

title: Unframed
overview: A short film anthology: Park Jung-min's "Class President Election": An elementary school noir capturing the fierce class president election in 5th Grade Class 2, as intense as the adult world. Son Seok-gu's "Rebroadcast": A road movie depicting the both annoying and tender day of an aunt and her nephew who end up attending a wedding together. Choi Hee-seo's "Bandi": The story of single mom So-young and her nine-year-old daughter Bandi, who decide to reveal a secret they've kept hidden until now. Lee Je-hoon's "Blue Happiness": The story of job-hunting graduate Chan-young, who dreams of an ordinary life while facing anxieties and worries about the future, chasing happiness that eludes him no matter how hard he tries.

title: Under the Shadow of Azkorri
overview: A visual essay which investigates the spaces of images, departing from a photo taken on the beach of Azkorri (Basque Country). Questioning how a change of perspective might alter the sensibility towards an image, the video evolves around positioning a gesture encountered in the movie Még kér a nép (Red Psalm, 1972). The director subsequently encounters this gesture in a photograph of Russian LGBTQIA+ activist Yelena Grigoryeva. She explores that particular symbol by drawing lines to her own queer sexuality, to the aesthetic representation of communism, as well as to questions of dispossession and appropriation.

title: Unspoken
overview: Yerin, a high school girl who smokes in the street and wants to pretend to be strong has a run-in on her way to school.

title: Under the Same Sky
overview: Behind you lies the sea; before you stands the enemy. This is the reality for the people of Gaza—and for Palestinian journalist Sami, who records it all with his 360° camera in the VR documentary Under the Same Sky. Driving along the Mediterranean coast in a press vehicle, he documents the war in the Gaza Strip. On one side are peaceful waters; on the other, heart-wrenching ruins.

title: Unbearable
overview: When Alexander Romanov was a student at the Faculty of History at Saratov University and was fond of the works of Vladimir Lenin, he came to the conclusion that the idea of socialism was distorted in the Soviet Union. An attempt to convey this idea to others led him to the Mordovian camps.

title: Unknown
overview: A Russian boy who was raised by Wahhabis talks about his past.

title: Unborn
overview: After the loss of her child, María sinks into a deep solitude while her husband, José, spends most of his time away from home. In the midst of her grief, she begins listening to the broadcasts of a radio preacher who speaks of the coming of the Antichrist.

title: Untitled #5: A Tune of Nones at Noon
overview: A double love tribute: to the final film by Yasujiro Ozu (1903-1963) ‘An Autumn Afternoon’ or ‘Sanma no Aji’ (1962) and ‘A Rising Sun That Painted My Horizon' (2018). An archival re-appropriation cine-poem. A found footage haiku.

title: Under the Sky With No Mother
overview: 13-year-old Kim Young-chul, who reads his mother and is ill, has a father and two younger brothers who cannot afford to live. Neighbors who feel pity for this and sympathize with Dong-jang take the center stage and decide on her stepmother, so that Yeong-chul marries her father. However, the family faces difficulties due to the rude behavior of his younger brother Yeong-i. Her stepmother takes all of her and takes care of the poor children with her patience and sincerity. Eventually, order is established in her home, her laughter spreads, and her children gradually understand her mother.

title: Under the Light Bulb
overview: The little Fly lived happily and carefree, surrounded by the light of light bulbs and the love of her grandfather Silkworm. However, when the grandfather leaves the heroine, the light bulbs go with him. Now the Fly has to figure out what makes the light bulbs burn.

title: Unbounded Family
overview: This is the story of a Russian family who in September 2020, after several months of quarantine and online training, sold their only house in Oslo, closed their mortgage and rented a yacht for a year to go on a trip with their three sons (9, 7 and 5 years old). In the middle of their 10-month regatta in Lisbon, they were experiencing a tipping point - to move on or stop. This story is about how to stop being afraid and start living your life, and not the life of your children, how to escape from a pandemic, lockdowns and restrictions. And how to re-learn to enjoy the little things, like water in the shower or the absence of mold on the ceiling.

title: Unusual Day with White Skin
overview: Short 8mm film by Toshihiko Omote.

title: Unhappy
overview: A person is facing severe difficulties, a stranger helps him to escape. He expressed gratitude, but for some reason the girl was very upset.

title: Underdog
overview: Obviously a loser, our hero has been leading the life of an "underdog" for the last 5 years. No girlfriend, no career growth, just a cup of coffee in the morning makes his day more bearable. After meeting with his sworn friend Tanaka, our hero begins to doubt the meaning of his existence.

title: Ulsan Vulture
overview: In the winter of 2025, vulture chick came to Ulsan from Mongolia. People feed vultures who have no food, treat injured individuals, and return them to nature. How should vultures, who have lost their homes due to industrial development, live in the future?

title: Underworld Lamp
overview: The king, separated from his group during the hunt, finds himself in a cave, where the lamps of life are lit and extinguished. He is trying to prolong his son's life.

title: Unpublished Album
overview: A documentary about the enthusiastic photographer Igor Kostin, who worked at the Chornobyl disaster site.

title: Ulagai Maattru
overview: This stirring Tamil motivational anthem is a battle cry for resilience, hope, and unshakable determination in the face of adversity. With vivid metaphors and electrifying lyrics, it portrays life as a journey of peaks and valleys, compelling listeners to rise above despair. More than just a song—it’s a revolution. It speaks to the dreamers, the warriors, and the weary, reminding them that no struggle lasts forever and that courage can move mountains. Its message transcends boundaries: Keep fighting. The world bends when you refuse to break.  Ashok Saravanan’s composition masterfully blends driving percussion, soaring vocals, and anthemic melodies to amplify the track’s fiery spirit. The powerful refrain—"When sorrow comes, don’t stop!"—echoes like a mantra, cementing its timeless call to perseverance.

title: Ugly Cute
overview: An ugly boy has only six days to live, so he decides to spend them making his dreams come true. Can he do it? An ironic story that proves death is not the end.

title: UNDEAD MARCH
overview: Can the immortal soul live in the body of immortality? The existence of the soul and its immutability can be equal to the body and soul. Various themes are followed by a metaphor called a movie.

title: Under the Sun
overview: Under the Sun tells the story of the life choices of an acclaimed writer. He is on his way to somewhere, maybe into the night, or perhaps into the light. As memories start to flood into his mind, he has to face the consequences of his choices prematurely. And perhaps make a new one. Amor fati.

title: Untitled
overview: This video, in which two men—one of them in a suit, the other naked—engage in their daily morning routine in a shared bathroom, was made by Radim Labuda as his first video performance. Labuda’s early work is characterized by an interest in the dynamics of partner relations as well as broader social relations, in the representation of the power relations of domination and subordination but also subversion. Representations of physicality, including nudity, can imply self-acceptance, sincerity, and decisiveness or at other times inadequacy and insecurity, depending on the context.

title: Under the Split Light
overview: Hakka, a special and little-known ethnic group in Hainan, is a branch of the Chinese Hakka system that has been neglected. They are far from the mainland, and they are rarely mentioned. The relatively closed environment has allowed the Central Plains culture to be completely preserved. After that, it has formed a special change with the ethnic minorities such as Li Miao. However, this unique traditional culture is now fading away in the erosion of modern civilization.

title: Under Construction
overview: Documentary about a worker who spent his entry life constructing building.

title: Underneath
overview: Beijing impresses people with its great richness of historical culture and the flourishing growth of its modern economy. But isn't this in itself a beautiful fantasy that hides the reality of Beijing life? A pervasive flood of images and forms have become a substitute for something else, and they completely fill our world. A tide of media images pour in on us from every direction, overfilling our eyes and ears, rendering our real senses insensate, allowing no line of sight to the reality of life, leaving us powerless. Hussling and bustling, people just rush by it all, and in the flourish of today's consumer culture, just reach out and grab what they want for themselves.

title: Upside Dxown
overview: My sister Angelina is sitting on the swing and thinking: ‘What is the purpose of my life?’ She has already encountered unrequited love, the death of a guinea pig and a friendly betrayal. Angelina considered herself cute two years ago, but now she is not confident in her appearance. Her hair is greasy, her breasts are not growing, her teeth are crooked. Everything is as it should be for a girl in her early teens. But my sister does not despair and does not want to change for someone.

title: Until the Day of Liberation: Retracing Korean Japanese History
overview: Shin Ki-soo spent his life collecting visual materials, and drew upon them to make this film about the history of zainichi Koreans in pre-war Japan. From the massacre of Koreans at the construction site of the Nakatsugawa hydroelectric power plant in Niigata Prefecture and the joint struggle of Korean and Japanese factory girls at the Kishiwada cotton mills to the dispute at the Sanshin Railroad construction site in Aichi Prefecture and the strike at the Aso coal mines in the Chikuho region of Kyushu, this film uncovers and assembles valuable testimony from many people who played key roles in the movement to free Korea from Japanese colonialism.

title: Undersea Imprisonment
overview: A lugubrious man hired to do a crime, now awaits for his last, brief contact with the mobsters, to pick-up his money. A very short film which tries to re-imagine film gris and to capture the sub-genre's "greater psychological and social realism".

title: Until We Meet Again
overview: Two strangers, victims of a mountain climbing accident, await rescue by getting to know one another and eventually making love. The woman becomes pregnant, but has no hope of contacting the father of her child. Seven years later, her sister introduces her fiance to the woman and it is the same man that she met earlier on the mountain.

title: Under the Rubble
overview: Under the Rubble is the filmmakers’ harrowing attempt to tell the real story behind the 1982 Israeli invasion of Lebanon as it took place in Beirut—a traumatizing experience for the city and its people. This moving and informative documentary won the Special Jury Award at the Valencia Film Festival.

title: Umar-ako
overview: Umarali Sattorov is a noble man who was born and lives in Konibodom city. Duringthe time of the Soviet Union he lived a life according to the human values of love and loyalty.And now he wants to establish himself in the new era of today’s economics. But…

title: Under Control
overview: João and Maria have a quiet life, focused on their routines, until they realize that the world they live in is a show. While Maria struggles to escape the manipulation, the line between the real and the imaginary begins to be broken. Not everything is under control.

title: UNAUTHORIZED PEOPLE
overview: 8mm graduation project. A psychological short story set in a world of ruins.  A unique film that combines elements of experimental cinema with drama, such as the use of color filters and re-shot footage from a cathode-ray tube.

title: Up in the Wind
overview: In the face of his homosexual identity, the passing away of a loved one, and the changes in his surroundings, Yang holds up his camera to try to find a way out, despite his confusion, frustration, and anxiety.

title: Under
overview: A short story of a man who, while expecting, became something unexpected.

title: Until You Have  Beauty: Plastic Surgery
overview: If your face is ugly, you can't do it all! Closely attached to the woman who realized this truth. Surgeon Asami of Asami Cosmetic Surgery performed the operation himself, and the entire surgical scene is completely recorded! This is a true cosmetic surgery video!

title: Uzu no Tatsumaki
overview: The story of a little boy. Adults are told that "Mom is fighting evil spirits in the hospital." He believes he will never lose because he is a strong mom, but mom can't leave the hospital at all. .. Finally, the boy decides to defeat the evil spirit and challenges the world within himself.

title: Until the Government Do Us Apart
overview: Different testimonies of the LGBT + community and several activists of the same group unleash different stories that give a broad panorama to Mexican society on topics of sexual diversity and equal marriages.

title: Under the Sea
overview: A mother, Soon-hee, and her son, Hae-seok, lived in a village for a long time. One day, they are told that sea levels have risen and that they have to leave the village. They begin to learn how to swim together to stay in the village. Meanwhile, Soon-hee's friend Bok-young gives Soon-hee something to help her swim...

title: Unrest
overview: The local government of Liaoning province restricted the fishing time for jellyfish on one of the days between July 15th and 30th, this is the documentary on those fishermen waiting the confirmed date to come.

title: Umuijishi
overview: The film follows wood sculptor Nakasone Masahiro's techniques and thoughts on creating a new replica of the lion of Shitahaku and Shitahaku people's thoughts on their lion dance.

title: Urdo on the Dead Wood
overview: In the depths of winter, the Tuomao people follow their herds across the grasslands. Kaikaizi and his mother had pitched their tent on the meadows near Wild Bull Gulch Township. But during the journey, Kaikaizi was struck by the devastating news of his mother's passing.  Though the Tuomao share customs with the Tibetans, they practice Islam. In accordance with their faith, the deceased must be cleansed and prayed over by an imam on the very day of their death. Thus, Kaikaizi set out alone for Wild Bull Gulch Township in search of an imam.

title: Uniform
overview: “Our society is not ready for democracy,” one of the Internal Troops of Ukraine servicemen says on the Maidan in 2014. For the first time, those standing on the other side of the barricades are speaking up on the film screen. The citizens whose profession is to protect the democratic rule in the state, and who were unable to talk openly due to their uniforms. In this chronicle of the Kyiv confrontation and interviews, the director tries to find out how the people under uniforms are dealing with their right to choose, upon which democracy is based, while the war in eastern Ukraine unfolds.

title: Untitled
overview: Short film for the "A Commercial for Myself" programme.

title: Unloading of Coal
overview: Workers unload the coal they collected as part of their routine.

title: USA: danger from the right. Demoniac from Wisconsin
overview: Professor Valentin Zorin, political observer of the USSR State Television and Radio Broadcasting, talks about Joseph McCarthy, an American politician, a senator from Wisconsin, who held an extremely anti-communist position, who advocated an intensification of the Cold War with the USSR. The name of McCarthy is associated with a reactionary trend in the political life of the United States of the early 1950s, dubbed "McCarthyism" and consisted in the persecution of people only suspected of sympathizing with communism and not committing any crimes.

title: Utsunomiya tsuritenjô
overview: Japanese horror movie from 1921.

title: Utsunomiya tsuritenjô
overview: Japanese horror movie from 1915.

title: Utsunomiya tsuritenjô
overview: Japanese horror movie from 1919.

title: Underground
overview: Underground, directed by Abdulrahman Sandokji, sheds light on musical talents in the kingdom and examines the reality of the local music industry.

title: Unknown
overview: There are many "street barber stalls" under Hutoushan. Most of these stalls serve senior citizens and working people, and have become a traditional part of ordinary people's lives. The stall holder and the customer are separated by mesh nets, while friendships and a feeling of warmth and humanity flow between them.

title: Until the Last Breath
overview: In China’s Northeast, there is the Fuzhou Folk Music, a country music played with suona, big suona, double pipe, Zheng and percussion instruments. Song Xiping, a suona professor, is one of the main inheritors of this music.

title: Unforgettable
overview: Exploring the deserted city of Pripyat after the disaster.

title: Underwater: Merpeople
overview: Alex hides a part of his identity that sets him apart from others. But when an unexpected incident exposes everything, he shrinks under the gaze of the world. As he tries to conceal himself in shame and fear, someone unforeseen quietly reaches out a hand.

title: Upside Down Tree
overview: Can a tree planted upside down grow? A short documentary about Oknha Son Kuy and Khmer Krom people.

title: Unknown Raikin. On The War Roads
overview: A biographic documentary about Soviet actor Arkady Raikin which for the most part covers his artistic activities during the Great Patriotic War and the performances of his frontline brigade. Arkady Raikin is remembered by his children Konstantin and Ekaterina Raikins and his grandson Alexei.

title: Unripe Grape
overview: A group of teenagers going astray

title: Under My Skin
overview: An animadoc revealing the theme of life with severe acne and the hero's acceptance of himself.

title: Ulcer in Mind
overview: This film shows that domestic violence is an urgent issue in Krola village, Ratanakiri. There, Ching Meth, a Kreung ethnic widower had suffered from her aggressive ex-husband who attacked her with weapons many times and even threatened to shoot her. Then, being left alone with a son and daughter, she works hard, but could not stay alone. And once again, she is married with another violent man. As her 21-year-old son, Nhap Thy, is traumatized he depends on his mother’s support. Also, Mett’s daughter has suffered from gender-based violence. At the age of only three years, another relative raped her. Thinking about the miserable life, Ching Meth cries a lot and finds hope in Christian religion.

title: Under the Nuclear Shadow
overview: In the northern part of Kharkiv city, 22 kilometers from the Ukrainian-Russian border, lies the verdant campus of Piatyhatky. Since 2022, this region housing nuclear facilities has been subjected to consistent Russian bombardment.  Amidst this ongoing threat of nuclear catastrophe, how do the residents endure? How do they perceive their future?  What prospects do scientists have in such an environment? How does warfare impact the natural world? What activities do children engage in? And what source of strength allows individuals to persevere?

title: Up to the Stars
overview: Dedicated to the Soviet-Astronauts

title: Ukrainians, We Are Saved!
overview: A music film about the revival of Ukrainian songwriting. Ukrainian artists and poets reflect on the place and prospects of the nation in history.

title: Utsunomiya tsuritanjô
overview: Another version of the historical ghost story.

title: Unprovoked home
overview: Gadan Mansion is the place where the director has lived so far since she was born. She has suffered family conflicts that have become commonplace such as adultery, private loans and poverty. Such unhappiness, which lasted more than 10 years, gave her suicidal impulses. She begins to see her grandmother who always lives with energy. She is wondering how the grandma could live like that so decides to take a video with her camera.

title: Up Down
overview: Where there's an up, there's a down. That's how things go.

title: Urinal - An Ugly Film
overview: A rat goes to the urinal to pee

title: Unknown Species
overview: The heroine, Lili, is a young single mother who gave birth to her son, Little Taozi, after an unexpected pregnancy eight years ago. They live in a compound on the outskirts of Beijing, and Little Taozi rarely even goes to school. One day, Xiao-mei, a deceased friend who re-established contact by chance, comes to visit the family, and in the course of a day and a night of getting to know and spend time with them, Lili reveals the child's life and her own emotional life. In the days that follow, the secret of the child's father seems to be about to become known when, thanks to Xiao-mei's presence, the young father, Eldest Taozi, whom she has never met, suddenly appears in the heroine's social circle.

title: Under The Clouds
overview: Walter the lamb wants more than anything to become a free and beautiful cloud. But the path to his dream turns out to be really difficult and unpredictable.

title: Unscripted, Unaltered
overview: Months ago, an academic initiative was created in which the participants had to film a documentary. Juan decided to make it about himself, which led him to discover more about his place in art, and art's place in his life.

title: Ukrainization
overview: An animated propaganda film depicting the curiosities of Ukrainization in the 1920s in a satirical form.  It was prepared in the animation workshop of VUFKU in the spring of 1927 under the leadership of Levandovsky as his third work using paper dolls after "The Tale of the Straw Bull" and "The Tale of the Squirrel-Housewife and the Mouse-Villain" and was not planned for wide release.  The film is lost.

title: Under the Load of Life
overview: In the desolate mountains of Iran, over 20,000 people have become kolbars, forced into a dangerous smuggling trade which left 45 dead and 172 injured in 2022. This documentary focuses on opportunity and safety as basic rights as kolbars risk their lives daily and are frequently shot by Iranian border guards.

title: Unveiling of Bust of Mrs. van Reenen-Völter
overview: Reportage about the unveiling of the bust of Marie Amalie Dorothea Reenen-Völter (5 March 1854 - 10 July 1925), wife of the Mayor and publicist for the town of Bergen, North Holland.

title: Uchitel as a Vocation
overview: The film is dedicated to the artistic journey of director Alexei Uchitel, who began as a documentary filmmaker (“Rock”, “Obvodny Canal”), made a name with his debut feature films “Giselle’s Mania”, “His Wife’s Diary”, distinguished himself with experimental films “The Stroll”, “Dreaming of Space”, “Tsoi” and multi-budget “The Edge” and “Mathilde”. In the film dedicated to his 70th anniversary, when it is already possible to formulate some results, his favorite artists will help to reveal the endless variety of his creative palette: Yu. Shevchuk, O. Budina, E. Tsyganov, G. Tyunina, E. Mironov, V Mashkov, Y. Peresild, D. Kozlovsky, I. Vernik, S. Garmash. The film uses archival materials of the author, who visited (with a camera) almost all the film sets of his protagonist, whose comments on his new projects will speckle the whole film.

title: Umaji Naik
overview: Directed by Gajanan Jagirdar.

title: Ukraine On Fire
overview: This film presents isolated episodes of Ukrainian life, such as life under Polish occupation (pacification), the “underground“, the tragedy in Rotterdam, the uprising of Carpathian Ukraine, large national demonstrations, Polish terrorist acts in Carpathian Ukraine...

title: Ukrainian Activists in Donetsk
overview: Chronicles April 2014 Ukrainian protests against Russian occupation intentions in Donetsk, Ukraine.

title: Under the Eyelids
overview: The space of sleep inside and outside the heroine, her fears and personal metamorphoses.

title: Unique Warrior
overview: An invisible hero, a warrior with righteousness, has an earth-shattering career of killing corrupt officials.

title: Unemployed Are Cooking for the Unemployed
overview: Ella Bergmann-Michel′s second movie "Erwerbslose kochen für Erwerbslose" was a assignment of the association of Frankfurt’s "Erwerblosenküchen" that during the world economy crisis could no longer serve meals for thousands of unemployed persons. The film was shown as a supporting film in many Frankfurt cinemas as well as in outdoor screenings at Frankfurt′s Hauptwache.

title: Us, the sea and the sad end
overview: When you finally arrive at your favorite snorkeling spot but then suddenly everything is getting out of control.  But do you know why?  A very short story in three acts.

title: Unspoken
overview: Unspoken or إحكِ is about Palestinian spoken word poet Farah Chamma who, at the age of 17, became an online sensation after her poems went viral. Today, Farah finds her poetry at odds with the world she lives in. The film shows her conflict as she struggles with self-censorship and keeping her poetry authentic and real.

title: Uncle
overview: Yonghan continues to run the porn theater that his family owns, even though business isn’t too good. He has a nice girlfriend — and a complicated past. When his father and his little daughter unexpectedly visit him in Seoul, Yonghan’s life is turned upside down.

title: Under the Basque Sky
overview: French travelogue about the coastal region of the Basque Country, with shots of San Sebastián and the area between Hendaia and Hondarribia.

title: Ukrainian Scientists in the Fight Against the Plague (1982)
Until Next Sunday
overview: Built as a flow of thought inspired by the concept of interior monologue by filmmaker Sergei Eisenstein, the documentary rescues the fragmented memory of a man who lived his childhood and adolescence during the military dictatorship, when it was common to beat his children at home and also in schools.

title: Under the Moon
overview: After her dog dies, Jiang Xinyue, a young Muslim woman who has repeatedly refused arranged blind dates, argues with her family. Caught between family pressure and personal desires, Jiang Xinyue chooses to bury her dog alone at night.

title: Umbrella
overview: A futuristic satire about a proud inventor and his discovery, an umbrella for every situation.

title: Underground City
overview: The story of Yoni and Nadav - two friends with trust issues, Boom and Ghetto - two soldiers in a criminal organization, and Zomer and Teichner - a pair of corrupt policemen. The six enter a vortex of violence, lies, and drugs - due to a dramatic poker night where all the cards are revealed. The three duos lead us through the dark colors and shadows of an underground city, a crime neighborhood controlled by the Georgian Ido.

title: Unbearably Loud
overview: This film tells the stories of five Russian families who have lost children, mothers, fathers, and grandchildren to shelling. What secret memories do they hold of their departed loved ones? Who do they hold responsible for these deaths? And how do they view the world now? These are the heartfelt monologues of people who have endured the unthinkable yet still retain their capacity for compassion. In this moment, nothing is more important than their words. Over the two years of military action in Ukraine, the UN estimates that more than 10,000 civilians have died—and the shells continue to claim lives every day. Novaya journalists are unable to access Ukrainian territory due to censorship and cannot fully tell the stories of what is happening there.

title: Us and Our Elders
overview: Instructive pictures that show how to try to solve aging care.

title: Uh-puh!
overview: Danbi, who was notified of her breakup by her boyfriend Hyun-je, suggests swimming in the park to make the last memory. Thus, the two begin to carry water from their house to the park.
